commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=83e2b8cffb85586b077cbf6c171c9fee6455a31d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core hybrid result merging so overlapping keyword and vector chunks with different IDs share scores, with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +71. Total +115 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue provides a concrete trigram/CJK configuration, and current source shows how an exact-ID mismatch plus `minScore` filtering loses the keyword contribution; no live reproduction was independently run during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] An open linked PR already owns the same repair, while this draft needs contributor proof and a maintainer choice between conflicting score semantics rather than another automated fix branch.

target.title: fix(memory-core): preserve overlapping hybrid keyword scores
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes hybrid memory search scoring for cases where FTS keyword results and vector results refer to overlapping memory ranges but use different chunk ids.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: hybrid memory search no longer drops keyword textScore when FTS and vector retrieval identify overlapping chunks with different ids.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at /Users/stellarfish/openclaw-matrix-proof on branch codex/memory-hybrid-overlap.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "preserves keyword-only hybrid hits when minScore exceeds text weight"; pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts; node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts; git diff --check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts.
Evidence after fix: hybrid helper regression passed 7 tests; memory index hybrid search target passed 1 test with 11 skipped; formatter, lint, and diff whitespace checks passed.
Observed result after fix: overlapping keyword/vector ranges merge into one result carrying both vectorScore and textScore, while disjoint same-file ranges remain separate.
What was not tested: live CJK memory_search against a trigram FTS database with Ollama embeddings.

## Changes

- Merge keyword textScore into same source/path vector results when line ranges overlap and ids differ.
- Keep exact chunk-id matching as the first merge path.
- Leave disjoint same-file keyword results separate to avoid unrelated score blending.
- Add regression coverage for overlapping-id drift and disjoint-range protection.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "preserves keyword-only hybrid hits when minScore exceeds text weight"
- pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts
- node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts
- git diff --check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts

## Risks

- Does not attempt semantic similarity matching; the fallback is scoped to line overlap only.
